 {"id":"git_safety_guard-hi1t","title":"[TASK] Production monitoring and observability for packs","description":"# Production Monitoring and Observability for Packs

## Problem
Once packs are deployed, we need to know:
- Are packs actually blocking dangerous commands?
- Are there false positives frustrating users?
- Which packs are most used/triggered?
- Are there patterns that never match (dead code)?

## Deliverables

### 1. Opt-In Telemetry
User-configurable telemetry (OFF by default):
```toml
[telemetry]
enabled = true
anonymize = true  # Hash command content
endpoint = 'https://dcg.dev/telemetry'
```

Collected metrics:
- Pack name triggered
- Pattern ID matched
- Block/allow decision
- Command hash (not content)
- Timestamp

### 2. Local Statistics
`dcg stats` command showing local history:
```
Pack Statistics (last 30 days):
  secrets.vault:     47 blocks, 0 allows, 2 bypasses
  backup.rclone:     12 blocks, 0 allows, 1 bypass
  Total:             142 blocks, 23 allows, 5 bypasses

Most triggered patterns:
  1. vault secrets disable (23 times)
  2. rsync --delete (12 times)
  3. aws s3 rb (8 times)
```

### 3. False Positive/Negative Tracking
Quick feedback mechanism:
```bash
# After a block, user can report
dcg feedback --false-positive 'vault list secret/'
dcg feedback --false-negative 'vault kv destroy secret/prod'
```

### 4. Health Check Endpoint
For CI/monitoring integration:
```bash
dcg health
# Returns: OK, pack count, pattern count, last update
```

### 5. Dead Pattern Detection
Identify patterns that never match:
```bash
dcg analyze --unused-patterns
# Reports patterns with 0 matches in production
```

## Privacy Considerations
- Command content NEVER sent to telemetry
- Only hashes and metadata
- User must explicitly opt-in
- Local stats stay local

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Telemetry system designed (opt-in)
- [ ] Local statistics command implemented
- [ ] Feedback mechanism implemented
- [ ] Health check endpoint available
- [ ] Dead pattern detection available
- [ ] Privacy review completed","notes":"Testing/Validation:
- Unit tests for metrics/log formatting and redaction behavior.
- Integration test to ensure telemetry can be enabled/disabled deterministically.
- E2E harness captures sample log/metric emission with verbose logging for audit.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:56:58.284194805-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T17:03:07.60832513-05:00"}

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-hxgx","title":"[TASK] Pack development and validation tooling","description":"# Pack Development and Validation Tooling

## Problem
Pack implementers need tools to:
- Validate their patterns work correctly
- Test patterns interactively
- Debug pattern matching issues
- Ensure patterns meet performance requirements

## Deliverables

### 1. Interactive Pattern Tester
`dcg dev test-pattern` command:
```bash
$ dcg dev test-pattern 'vault secrets disable .*'
Pattern: vault secrets disable .*
Type: destructive
Regex valid: ✓
Complexity score: 2 (good)

Test commands:
  ✓ 'vault secrets disable foo' -> BLOCKED
  ✓ 'vault secrets enable foo' -> ALLOWED
  ✓ 'vault list' -> ALLOWED

Enter test command (or 'q' to quit):
> vault secrets disable production
Result: BLOCKED (matched pattern)
```

### 2. Pack Validator
`dcg dev validate-pack <path>`:
```bash
$ dcg dev validate-pack src/packs/secrets/vault.rs

Validating secrets.vault pack...

Structure:
  ✓ keywords defined
  ✓ destructive_patterns present
  ✓ safe_patterns present

Patterns:
  ✓ 8 destructive patterns
  ✓ 4 safe patterns
  ✓ No duplicate patterns
  ✓ No overly broad patterns
  ✓ Regex complexity OK

Tests:
  ✓ Unit tests present (12 tests)
  ✓ Coverage: 94%
  ✓ All tests passing

Performance:
  ✓ Pack evaluation: 234µs (< 500µs limit)
  ✓ Pattern matching: 12µs avg

Documentation:
  ✓ Reference doc exists
  ⚠ Missing example for 'vault token revoke'

Overall: PASS (1 warning)
```

### 3. Pattern Debugger
`dcg dev debug` for troubleshooting:
```bash
$ dcg dev debug 'vault secrets disable foo'

Command: vault secrets disable foo

Pack evaluation trace:
  [secrets.vault] Checking keywords... MATCH (vault)
  [secrets.vault] Checking safe patterns...
    - 'vault read' -> NO MATCH
    - 'vault list' -> NO MATCH
  [secrets.vault] Checking destructive patterns...
    - 'vault secrets disable' -> MATCH at position 0-21
    
Result: BLOCKED by secrets.vault
Reason: 'vault secrets disable' permanently disables a secrets engine
Pattern: vault secrets disable
```

### 4. Benchmark Runner
`dcg dev benchmark`:
```bash
$ dcg dev benchmark secrets.vault

Benchmarking secrets.vault pack...

Pattern matching (1000 iterations):
  vault secrets disable foo:  45µs ± 3µs
  vault kv delete secret/x:   52µs ± 4µs
  vault read secret/x:        38µs ± 2µs

Full pack evaluation:  234µs ± 15µs

Comparison to baseline:
  Current: 234µs
  Baseline: 220µs
  Delta: +14µs (+6.4%)
  Status: PASS (< 10% regression threshold)
```

### 5. Fixture Generator
`dcg dev generate-fixtures`:
```bash
$ dcg dev generate-fixtures secrets.vault

Generating test fixtures for secrets.vault...

Created:
  tests/fixtures/secrets_vault_destructive.yaml (8 commands)
  tests/fixtures/secrets_vault_safe.yaml (4 commands)
  tests/fixtures/secrets_vault_edge_cases.yaml (6 commands)
  scripts/e2e_tests/secrets_vault.txt (18 test cases)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Interactive pattern tester implemented
- [ ] Pack validator implemented
- [ ] Pattern debugger implemented
- [ ] Benchmark runner implemented
- [ ] Fixture generator implemented
- [ ] All tools documented in developer guide","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:56:59.271875923-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:11:41.136818804-05:00"}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-i79","title":"[TASK] Create pack unit test template and guidelines","description":"# Pack Unit Test Template and Guidelines

## Scope
Create a reusable template and documentation for writing unit tests for new packs.

## Deliverables

### 1. Test Template File
Create `src/packs/test_template.rs` with:
```rust
// Template structure for pack unit tests
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    
    // Test each destructive pattern
    #[test]
    fn test_destructive_pattern_X_matches() { ... }
    
    // Test each safe pattern
    #[test]
    fn test_safe_pattern_Y_allows() { ... }
    
    // Test edge cases
    #[test]
    fn test_empty_command() { ... }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_special_characters() { ... }
    
    // Test non-matching (specificity)
    #[test]
    fn test_unrelated_command_not_matched() { ... }
}
```

### 2. Testing Guidelines Document
Create `docs/pack-testing-guide.md` with:
- Minimum test coverage requirements
- How to test regex patterns
- How to test safe vs destructive classification
- Common edge cases to always test
- How to add test fixtures

### 3. Test Assertion Helpers
Create helper functions in `src/packs/test_helpers.rs`:
- `assert_blocks(pack, command, expected_reason)`
- `assert_allows(pack, command)`
- `assert_pattern_matches(pattern, input, expected)`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Template file created with full example
- [ ] Guidelines document covers all scenarios
- [ ] Helper functions implemented and documented
- [ ] At least one existing pack updated to use new template as example

## Logging Requirements
- Test failures should log the full pattern and input
- Log which specific pattern matched/failed
- Include timing information for pattern matching","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:09:27.958799119-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T21:31:41.93405328-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T21:31:41.93405328-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: Created pack testing infrastructure with test_helpers.rs (10 assertion functions), test_template.rs (23 example tests), pack-testing-guide.md documentation, and updated core.git with comprehensive tests using the new framework. All 149 pack tests pass."}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","title":"[TEMPLATE] Standard Pack Task Requirements","description":"# Standard Pack Task Requirements Template

## Purpose
This template defines what EVERY pack task must include. Reference this when creating new pack tasks or validating existing ones.

## Required Sections for Every Pack Task

### 1. Scope (Required)
Brief description of what tool/CLI/API this pack protects.

### 2. Destructive Patterns (Required)
List ALL destructive patterns with:
- Exact command/pattern
- Clear explanation of why it's dangerous
- Example of data loss or damage it could cause

### 3. Safe Patterns (Required)  
List safe patterns that should NOT be blocked:
- Read-only operations
- Status/info commands
- Dry-run/preview modes

### 4. Implementation Notes (Required)
- Parent category bead ID
- Keywords to trigger pack evaluation
- Special considerations (aliases, API endpoints, etc.)
- Known overlaps with other packs

### 5. Testing Requirements (Required)
Standard checklist for ALL packs:
- [ ] Unit tests in `src/packs/<category>/<pack>.rs`
- [ ] E2E tests in `scripts/e2e_tests/<pack>.txt`
- [ ] Test coverage >= 90%
- [ ] All destructive patterns have test cases
- [ ] All safe patterns have test cases
- [ ] Edge cases tested (quotes, special chars)
- [ ] Performance benchmark < 500µs

### 6. Acceptance Criteria (Required)
Specific, measurable criteria:
- [ ] Pattern X blocked
- [ ] Pattern Y allowed
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] Completion checklist passed

## Example Structure
```markdown
# [PACK] category.name - Tool Name Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for ToolName CLI operations.

## Destructive Patterns
- \`tool delete X\` - Permanently deletes X
- \`tool remove --force\` - Force removes without confirmation

## Safe Patterns
- \`tool list\`, \`tool show\`
- \`tool status\`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: category.* (git_safety_guard-XXX)
- Keywords: tool
- Note: Consider --dry-run flag

## Testing Requirements
[Standard checklist as above]

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Delete operations blocked
- [ ] List/show operations allowed
- [ ] Completion checklist passed
```

## Validation
Use [CHECKLIST] Pack Implementation Completion Validation (git_safety_guard-XXXX) to validate each pack.","notes":"# Standard Pack Task Requirements Template

## Purpose
This template defines what EVERY pack task must include. Reference this when creating new pack tasks or validating existing ones.

## Required Sections for Every Pack Task

### 1. Scope (Required)
Brief description of what tool/CLI/API this pack protects.

### 2. Destructive Patterns (Required)
List ALL destructive patterns with:
- Exact command/pattern
- Clear explanation of why it's dangerous
- Example of data loss or damage it could cause

- Dry-run/preview modes

- Keywords to trigger pack evaluation
- Special considerations (aliases, API endpoints, etc.)

### 5. Testing Requirements (Required)
Standard checklist for ALL packs:
- [ ] Unit tests in `src/packs/<category>/<pack>.rs`
- [ ] E2E tests in `scripts/e2e_tests/<pack>.txt`
- [ ] E2E runs use the shared harness (git_safety_guard-ksk.1.4) with `--verbose` JSON logging
- [ ] Test coverage >= 90%
- [ ] All destructive patterns have test cases
- [ ] All safe patterns have test cases
- [ ] Edge cases tested (quotes, special chars)
- [ ] Performance benchmark < 500µs

### 6. Acceptance Criteria (Required)
Specific, measurable criteria:
- [ ] Pattern X blocked
- [ ] Pattern Y allowed
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] Completion checklist passed

## Example Structure
```markdown
# [PACK] category.name - Tool Name Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for ToolName CLI operations.

## Destructive Patterns
- `tool delete X` - Permanently deletes X
- `tool remove --force` - Force removes without confirmation

## Safe Patterns
- `tool list`, `tool show`
- `tool status`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: category.* (git_safety_guard-XXX)
- Keywords: tool
- Note: Consider --dry-run flag

## Testing Requirements
[Standard checklist as above]

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Delete operations blocked
- [ ] List/show operations allowed
- [ ] Completion checklist passed
```

## Validation

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jbg","title":"[PACK] loadbalancer.elb - AWS ELB Pack","description":"# AWS ELB/ALB/NLB Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for AWS Elastic Load Balancer operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### ELBv2 (ALB/NLB)
- `aws elbv2 delete-load-balancer`
- `aws elbv2 delete-target-group`
- `aws elbv2 deregister-targets`
- `aws elbv2 delete-listener`
- `aws elbv2 delete-rule`

### Classic ELB
- `aws elb delete-load-balancer`
- `aws elb deregister-instances-from-load-balancer`

## Safe Patterns
- `aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers`
- `aws elbv2 describe-target-groups`
- `aws elbv2 describe-target-health`
- `aws elb describe-load-balancers`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: loadbalancer.* (git_safety_guard-16f)
- Keywords: aws, elbv2, elb
- Note: Target deregistration affects live traffic

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Load balancer deletion blocked
- [ ] Target group deletion blocked
- [ ] Target deregistration blocked
- [ ] Tests cover both ELBv2 and Classic","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:34:14.997443776-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T14:34:14.997443776-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jbg","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:37.903224595-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jbg","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:37.945502832-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jbg","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:37.988808473-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jbg","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.030254364-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jbg","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.075442436-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jbg","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.114119888-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jbg","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.181860284-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jbg","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.227878907-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-o6p","title":"Create dependabot.yml configuration file","description":"# Task: Create dependabot.yml Configuration File

## What to Do

Create `.github/dependabot.yml` to enable automated dependency updates for both Cargo dependencies and GitHub Actions.

## File Content

Create `.github/dependabot.yml`:

```yaml
# Dependabot configuration for DCG
# Docs: https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/dependabot/dependabot-version-updates

version: 2

updates:
  # Cargo (Rust) dependencies
  - package-ecosystem: "cargo"
    directory: "/"
    schedule:
      interval: "weekly"
      day: "monday"
      time: "09:00"
      timezone: "America/New_York"
    open-pull-requests-limit: 5
    commit-message:
      prefix: "deps"
      include: "scope"
    labels:
      - "dependencies"
      - "rust"
    groups:
      # Group minor/patch updates together
      rust-minor-patch:
        patterns:
          - "*"
        update-types:
          - "minor"
          - "patch"
        exclude-patterns:
          - "serde*"
      # Keep serde updates separate (widely used, review carefully)
      serde:
        patterns:
          - "serde*"

  # GitHub Actions
  - package-ecosystem: "github-actions"
    directory: "/"
    schedule:
      interval: "weekly"
      day: "monday"
      time: "09:00"
      timezone: "America/New_York"
    open-pull-requests-limit: 3
    commit-message:
      prefix: "ci"
      include: "scope"
    labels:
      - "dependencies"
      - "github-actions"
    groups:
      actions:
        patterns:
          - "*"
```

## Configuration Explained

### Package Ecosystems
- **cargo**: Monitors Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock
- **github-actions**: Monitors workflow YAML files

### Schedule
- **Weekly on Monday 9am EST**: Regular cadence, start of week
- Gives time to review and merge before weekend

### PR Limits
- **5 for Cargo**: Reasonable number of Rust updates
- **3 for Actions**: Actions update less frequently

### Grouping Strategy
- **rust-minor-patch**: Groups all minor/patch updates except serde
- **serde**: Separate group because serde is core dependency
- **actions**: Group all GitHub Action updates together

### Labels
Auto-applied labels help with:
- Filtering PRs by type
- Automated workflows
- Tracking dependency debt

## Files to Create

- .github/dependabot.yml

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Cargo ecosystem configured
- [ ] GitHub Actions ecosystem configured
- [ ] Grouping strategy implemented

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-oien","title":"False-positive escape hatch + UX hardening","description":"Goal: reduce disruptive false positives in agentic coding while preserving safety guarantees and auditability.","notes":"Background:
- dcg must be conservative; some legitimate workflows get blocked.
- Users need a safe, auditable escape hatch that does not weaken global policy.
- The hook output can be truncated in agent TUIs, so critical escape information must be placed at the very top of the denial message.

Principles:
- Do not silently allow destructive commands. Exceptions must be explicit, scoped, and time-bounded.
- Preserve existing allow/deny semantics unless an explicit exception applies.
- Keep the UX fast and self-contained: one terminal command should be sufficient to grant a temporary exception.
- All exceptions must be logged, visible, and expire automatically.
- All unit + e2e coverage must reuse the existing regression harness and helpers (git_safety_guard-ksk.1.4 and git_safety_guard-ksk.1.5); no bespoke harnesses.

Definition of done:
- A short-code allow-once mechanism exists, is time-limited, scoped, and audited.
- Logging and tests (unit + e2e) prove behavior and expiry using the shared harness schema.
- Additional false-positive mitigation strategies are documented and gated by tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.529342484-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T16:52:39.347361116-05:00"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1","title":"Design allow-once short-code exception flow","description":"Specify the short-code scheme, storage, and UX for temporary exceptions.","notes":"Core idea:
- On every block, dcg prints a 4-char code at the very top of the denial message.
- Code = last 4 hex chars of SHA256 of: "<datetime> | <cwd> | <full command text>" (lowercase).
- Code maps to a pending exception record (full hash + command + cwd + timestamp).
- Exceptions expire after 24 hours and are auto-pruned.

Decisions (locked):
- Default semantics: allow-once is reusable until expiry for the exact command+cwd.
- Optional tighter mode: `--single-use` consumes the exception after the first allow.
- Precedence: allow-once overrides pack-based denies, but does NOT override explicit user-config blocklist patterns by default. Use `--force` (with extra confirmation) to override explicit blocklist if truly needed.
- Redaction: store raw command for exact match, but derive a redacted display string for list/log output by default.

Design details to capture:
- Timestamp format for hashing/logging (UTC, RFC3339, monotonic fallback).
- CWD definition (literal current_dir vs repo root) and how it is captured.
- Command canonicalization: raw command text as executed (including newlines and whitespace).
- Collision policy: 4-char suffix is for UI only; full hash stored; collisions require disambiguation on allow.
- Security policy: optional HMAC secret (env var) if needed to prevent forged codes.
- Scope enforcement: exceptions must be bound to cwd (or repo root) and command text.
- Storage location: user config dir (e.g., ~/.config/dcg/pending_exceptions.jsonl) plus ephemeral allowlist file.
- Management UX: list/clear/revoke pending and active temporary exceptions.

Acceptance criteria:
- Spec answers collision, scope, expiry, logging, canonicalization, and precedence questions.
- Includes exact CLI UX (command syntax, prompts, non-interactive flags).
- Defines output format for top-of-message code and JSON fields.

Testing/Validation Plan:
- Specify the unit-test matrix (hashing, expiry, scoping, collisions, redaction, precedence) and e2e flows, and map them to git_safety_guard-oien.1.6/1.7.
- Require shared harness usage (git_safety_guard-ksk.1.4/1.5) with explicit logging fields.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.577173153-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:32:20.511136604-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:32:20.511136604-05:00","close_reason":"Design spec drafted","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.589738585-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.1","title":"Pending exceptions store + expiry pruning","description":"Implement a persistent pending-exception record store with automatic 24h expiry.","notes":"Scope:
- Record created on block: code, full hash, timestamp, cwd, command (full text), and reason info.
- Storage format: JSONL or TOML with deterministic ordering; include schema version.
- Prune expired entries on load or on every write.
- Handle collisions by storing all entries for a suffix.
- Concurrency: ensure atomic append or lock to avoid corruption when multiple hooks run.
- Redaction: store raw command for exact matching, but also store a redacted display string for list/log output by default; never print raw unless --show-raw.
- Consumption: only track usage when a single-use exception is created; default reusable exceptions do not decrement.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add/lookup/prune functions are deterministic and tested.
- Expired (and consumed, if applicable) entries are removed automatically.
- Pending store is safe to read even if partially corrupted (fail-open).
- Logging includes when entries are pruned or consumed.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.633533311-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T16:56:55.595925037-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.635173751-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Consumption: only track usage when a single-use exception is created; default reusable exceptions do not decrement.

Acceptance criteria:

- Expired (and consumed, if applicable) entries are removed automatically.
- Pending store is safe to read even if partially corrupted (fail-open).

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.10","title":"Optional HMAC hardening for short codes","description":"Add optional HMAC secret to prevent forged allow-once codes.","notes":"Scope:
- If DCG_ALLOW_ONCE_SECRET is set, incorporate HMAC into the hash input.
- Maintain backwards-compatible behavior when the secret is unset.
- Document trade-offs: security vs recoverability if secret changes.
- Add tests gated on secret presence.

Acceptance criteria:
- When enabled, codes cannot be generated without the secret.
- When disabled, behavior matches baseline spec.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:34:15.647345959-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T16:40:55.312918218-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.10","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:34:15.648718394-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.10","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:37:22.100323771-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.2","title":"CLI: dcg allow-once <code>","description":"Provide a one-command UX to grant a temporary exception using the short code.","notes":"Scope:
- Command: `dcg allow-once <code>` (with --yes for non-interactive).
- Resolve code to pending record; if multiple, require disambiguation (list numbered options, support --pick). Default display uses redacted command; `--show-raw` required to print full command.
- Confirm by echoing exact command, cwd, and expiry timestamp.
- Support `--dry-run` and `--json` output for automation.
- Support `--single-use` to consume the exception after first allow (default is reusable until expiry).
- If the matching block is from explicit user-config blocklist, require `--force` plus extra confirmation.
- Write an ephemeral allowlist entry with expires_at = now+24h (and uses_remaining when single-use).

Acceptance criteria:
- User can copy the top-line code and allow the exact same command.
- Collisions are handled without ambiguity.
- Allowlist entry expires automatically and consumption is enforced when single-use.

Verification:
- Covered by git_safety_guard-oien.1.6 (unit) and git_safety_guard-oien.1.7 (e2e) using the shared harness/log schema.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.667348316-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T17:02:32.375117707-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.668595956-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:36:11.474798319-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.3","title":"Ephemeral allowlist layer + cwd scoping","description":"Add a temporary allowlist layer and enforce cwd/project scoping for allow-once entries.","notes":"Scope:
- Introduce an ephemeral allowlist layer with highest precedence among pack rules.
- Precedence: explicit user-config blocklist > allow-once > pack evaluation. Allow-once overrides pack-based denies only; use `--force` to override explicit blocklist.
- Allowlist entry includes scope conditions like `cwd` or `project_root`.
- Evaluation enforces scope before matching exact command.
- Expired entries are ignored and pruned; consumed entries are removed for single-use exceptions.

Acceptance criteria:
- Allow-once entry only matches in intended cwd (or project).
- Behavior is deterministic, audited in logs, and respects the chosen precedence policy.

Verification:
- Covered by git_safety_guard-oien.1.6 (unit) and git_safety_guard-oien.1.7 (e2e) using the shared harness/log schema.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.699271226-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T17:02:37.408621505-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.700499339-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:36:16.517427941-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
